pi_items.source: apps.kde.org GCompris page confirms Traffic as a real activity described as 'A sliding-block puzzle game'
Path used in setup_task.sh (line 12) and export_result.sh (line 12); standard Linux temporary file location
Actual verification code in verifier.py line 90 checks 'if size_kb > 5' (5KB minimum), not 10KB. The metadata value is never referenced in the verification logic.

GCompris activities support difficulty scaling; Traffic is confirmed as a sliding-block puzzle suitable for children aged 2-10. Task timeout of 300 seconds and max 50 steps are reasonable for a puzzle activity.

pi_items.metadata_value: Rush Hour-style sliding block puzzle (from task description)
pi_items.source: apps.kde.org GCompris screenshots page confirms: 'Traffic - A sliding-block puzzle game. Remove the red car from the parking lot through the gate on the right.' This matches Rush Hour puzzle mechanics.

pi_items.verified_value: Current version is 26.0 (February 2026); version 2.3 was from February 2022
pi_items.source: gcompris.net/downloads-en.html shows current version 26.0; task_utils.sh line 33 is outdated documentation

privileged_info_summary: GCompris is real educational software with 190 activities for children aged 2-10. The Traffic activity exists and is verified as a sliding-block puzzle game matching Rush Hour-style mechanics. The expected screenshot path /tmp/traffic_completed.png is correctly implemented in the task scripts. However, there is a factual contradiction: the metadata claims min_screenshot_size_kb of 10KB, but the actual verification code (verifier.py line 90) only requires 5KB minimum. The metadata value is never used by the verifier logic, indicating a documentation-code mismatch.
